name: 4xCompatMode
description: Allows compatibility with 4.x-based custom agents used as Credential Collectors. See Using Credential Collectors Between 4.x Type and Newer Type Agents.

name: AcceptTPCookie
description: Determines if the agent accepts cookies created by the SiteMinder SDK. See Configure Support for SDK Third-Party Cookies.

name: AgentConfigObject
description: (In WebAgent.conf) Determines which Agent Configuration Object that the agent should use. See Parameters Found Only in Local Configuration Files.

name: AgentName
description: Defines the identity of the agent. See Set the AgentName and DefaultAgentName Values

name: AgentNamesAreFQHostNames
description: Instructs an FCC or SCC to use the fully qualified host name in the target URL as the Agent name. See Configure Credential Collectors in a Mixed Environment.

name: AgentWaitTime
description: Determines how long to wait for the Policy Server when attaining the Host Configuration Object and Agent Configuration Object. See Accommodate Network Latency.

name: AllowCacheHeaders
description: Determines if the agent removes cache headers such as “if-modified-since” and “if-none-match.” See Control How HTTP Header Resources are Cached.

name: AllowLocalConfig
description: Enables local configuration. See Implement Local Configuration and Restrict Changes to Local Configuration Parameters.

name: AppendIISServerLog
description: Determines whether the the agent adds the Transaction ID to the IIS log. See Record the User Name and Transaction ID in IIS Server Logs.

name: AutoAuthorizeHttpMethods
description: Specifies HTTP methods that, if included in a request, that request is authorized without being challenging for credentials. See Configure Web Agent Single Sign-On Settings.

name: AutoAuthorizeOptions
description: Enables automatic authorization of requests for resources which use the HTTP OPTIONS method. See Allow Automatic Access to Resources that use the OPTIONS Method.

name: AuthorizationCacheTimeout
description: Controls the authorization of Agent cache independently. The values defined in this ACO property are used to initialize the cache. See Web Agent Caches.

name: BadCSSChars
description: A comma-separated list of characters to block due to use in XSS attacks. See Protect Web Sites Against Cross-Site Scripting.

name: BadFormChars
description: A comma-separated list of characters to block from use in forms. See Enable Bad Form Characters.

name: BadQueryChars
description: A comma-separated list of characters to block from use after the ? character. See Specify Bad Query Characters.

name: BadUrlChars
description: A comma-separated list of character sequences to block from use in URL requests to prevent exploitation by malicious web clients. See Specify Bad URL Characters.

name: CacheAnonymous
description: Specifies whether anonymous user information should be cached. See Cache Anonymous Users.

name: CCCExt
description: Specifies the extension of the Cookie Credential Collector. See Specify the Cookie Provider.

name: ClientLocalePreferrred
description: Specifies the preference order for localization. See Define the Configuration Modes Order of Precedence.

name: compatRealmtimeouts
description: Specifies whether the Policy Server challenges users for their credentials after a realm timeout occurs. See Prevent Re-Challenges After Realm Timeouts When Multiple Valid Sessions Exist.

name: ConformToRFC2047
description: Specifies whether the agent conforms to RFC 2047. See Default HTTP Headers Used by the Product.

name: ConformToRFC6265
description: Specifies whether a leading dot must be appended to a domain name when the value of CookieDomain is empty.

name: ConstructFullPwsvcUrl
description: Specifies whether the FQDN is used in Password Services redirect URLs. See Use a Fully Qualified URL for Password Services Redirects.

name: CookieDomain
description: Specifies the cookie domain of the agent. See Configure Web Agent Single Sign-On Settings.

name: CookieDomainScope
description: Specifies the number of sections (characters with periods between them) in the domain name. See Implement Cookie Domain Resolution.

name: CookiePath
description: Specifies the path for the primary-domain session cookies created by the cookie provider when CookiePathScope is not specified. See Specify the Cookie Path for Agent Cookies.

name: CookiePathScope
description: Specifies the scope of the cookie path for secondary agent cookies. See Specify the Cookie Path for Agent Cookies.

name: CookieProvider
description: Specifies the URL of the web server where the agent that is acting as the cookie provider resides. See Configure Web Agent Single Sign-On Settings.

name: CookieValidationPeriod
description: Specifies the time period (in seconds) in which the receiving agent accepts the session cookie. If not used or set to zero, it expires based on Idle/Max Timeout. See Protect Session Cookies from Misuse with Validation Periods and Expired Cookie URLs.

name: CORSConfiguration
description: Allows configuration of CORS filters such as allowed origins, headers, methods, and other custom headers on OIDC endpoints. See Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) Support for OIDC Endpoints.

name: CslCertUniqueAttribute
description: Lists the attributes of the certificate by which it is uniquely identified. See How to Link a Client Certificate to a Session (Windows).

name: CslMaxCacheEntries
description: Specifies the maximum number of entries that the agent cache contains. See How to Link a Client Certificate to a Session (Windows).

name: CSSChecking
description: Specifies whether the agent checks for Cross-Site Scripting. See Configure the Web Agent to Check For Cross-Site Scripting.

name: CSSErrorFile
description: Specifies the error file to use for error pages that result from HTTP 403 cross-site scripting errors. See How to Set Up Error Handling.

name: Custom401ErrorFile
description: Specifies the error file to use for error pages that result from HTTP 401 errors. See How to Set Up Error Handling.

name: CustomIpHeader
description: Specifies an HTTP header for which the agent searches to find the IP address of the requestor. See Configure IP Address Validation.

name: DecodeQueryData
description: Specifies whether the agent decodes query data before calling the Policy Server. See URL Settings.

name: DefaultAgentName
description: Specifies a name that the agent uses to process requests when no name is defined in the AgentName parameter. For more information, see Set the AgentName and DefaultAgentName Values.

name: DefaultHostName
description: Specifies the hostname to use when no host header is sent in the request. See Accommodate Testing Tools that do not send HOST Headers.

name: DefaultLocale
description: Specifies the language in which HTML pages for login, basic password services, and error responses are displayed. See FCC Internationalization.

name: DefaultPassword
description: (IIS only) Specifies the IIS Proxy Password to use. See Use an IIS Proxy User Account.

name: DefaultUsername
description: (IIS only) Specifies the IIS Proxy Account to use. See Use an IIS Proxy User Account.

name: DeleteCerts
description: (Apache only) Specifies whether the agent deletes certificates from Apache servers that use local files. See Delete Certificates from Stronghold Servers.

name: DisableAuthSrcVars
description: Specifies whether the agent should save HTTP header space by removing default header variables like SM_AUTHDIRNAME. See Disable Default HTTP Header Variables.

name: DisableDirectoryList
description: (Oracle iPlanet Web Server only) Specifies whether to disable directory listing. See Restrict Directory Browsing on a Oracle iPlanet Web Server.

name: DisableDNSLookup
description: Specifies whether to disable DNS lookups to help prevent DNS denial of service attacks. See Help Prevent DNS DOS Attacks.

name: DisableDotDotRule
description: Specifies whether the agent uses IgnoreExt on a URI where two periods (..) have a slash (/) between them. See Handle Complex URIs.

name: DisableI18N
description: (IIS only) If set, the NTC Credential Collector encodes URLs during redirects to protected resources. See How to Allow the NTC to Encode URLs During Redirects to Protected Resources.

name: DisablePostDataLimit
description: Specifies whether an agent observes the 64 KB data-size limit when preserving or filtering POST data. See (Optional) Disable the POST Preservation Data Size Limit.

name: DisableSessionVars
description: Specifies whether the agent should save HTTP header space by removing default header variables like SM_SERVERSESSIONID. See Disable Default HTTP Header Variables.

name: DisableUserNameVars
description: Specifies whether the agent should save HTTP header space by removing default header variables like SM_USER. See Disable Default HTTP Header Variables.

name: DisableWindowsSecurityContext
description: (IIS Only) If set, the agent ignores the Windows security context of the user. See Disable Windows Security Context on Agents for IIS.

name: DisallowUTF8NonCanonical
description: If set, prevents attackers from sending noncanonical (overlong) Unicode (utf-8) characters in requests and attempting to bypass cross-site scripting protection. See Protect J2EE Applications against Cross-Site Scripting Attacks.

name: DominoDefaultUser
description: (Domino agents only) Identifies a user with default access to the Notes database, which means this person has general access privileges. See Authenticate Users with the Domino Server

name: DominoLegacyDocumentSupport
description: (Domino agents only) Specifies how an agent handles user requests for protected Lotus Notes documents in a Domino environment. See Handle User-Requested Actions on Lotus Notes Documents

name: DominoLookUpHeaderForLogin
description: (Domino agents only) If set, instructs a Domino Web Agent to ask the Domino Web Server if the user requesting access to a resource is unique or ambiguous within the Domino user directory. See Use a SiteMinder Header for Authentication.

name: DominoMapUrlForRedirect
description: (Domino agents only) If set, for Domino view (.nsf) resources with a forms authentication scheme, map the URLs before they are redirected to the forms credential collector. See Map URLs for FCC Redirects with a Domino Web Agent.

name: DominoNormalizeUrls
description: (Domino agents only) If set, for Domino view (.nsf) resources with a forms authentication scheme, map the URLs before they are redirected to the forms credential collector. See Map URLs for FCC Redirects with a Domino Web Agent.

name: DominoSuperUser
description: (Domino agents only) Identifies a user who has access to all resources on the Domino server. See Authenticate as the Domino Super User.

name: DominoUseHeaderForLogin
description: (Domino agents only) Instructs a Domino Web Agent to pass the specified header value to the Domino Web Server. The Domino server uses the header data to identify a user in its user directory. See Use a SiteMinder Header for Authentication.

name: DominoUserForAnonAuth
description: (Domino agents only) Specifies a value for anonymous users. This value is sent to the Domino server when users access Domino resources that are protected with an anonymous authentication scheme. See Use an Anonymous Authentication Scheme with Domino.

name: enablecompression
description: (Sun ONE web server only) Specifies whether POST preservation is supported when compression is enabled on Sun ONE web server. Set the value to yes to enable the support.

name: EarlyCookieCommit
description: (IIS only) Specifies if cookies are set at an early point during processing (as needed for IIS ARR) or later during the “OnSendResponse request notification”. See Determine when the Agent for IIS Sets Cookies

name: EnableAccounting
description: Replaced by EnableAuditing. Maintained for backward compatibility.

name: EnableAuditing
description: Specifies whether the agent logs all successful authorizations that are stored in the user session cache. See Configure Auditing to Track User Activity.

name: EnableAuthorization
description: If set to No, the user is authenticated and granted access to the protected resource but SiteMinder does not authorize the user.Ignored when used with CA Data Protection or a cookie provider. See Manage Web Agent Authorization.

name: EnableCookieProvider
description: Specifies whether an agent processes requests form the cookie provider. See Disable Cookie Providers.

name: EnableFCCWindowsAuth
description: Specifies whether an agent, acting as an FCC, can authenticate users against resources that the Windows authentication scheme protects. See Configure the FCC to allow Windows authentication.

name: EnableFormCache
description: Specifies whether to use the forms template cache. See Enable the Form Cache.

name: EnableIntroscopeAgentSupport
description: Specifies whether to collect information about the agent and sends it to APM for SiteMinder using a plug-in. See Monitoring Web Agents.

name: EnableMonitoring
description: Specifies whether the agent sends monitoring information to the Policy Server. See Monitor Web Agents with the OneView Monitor.

name: EnableOtherAuthTrans
description: (Oracle iPlanet Web Server only) If set, allows additional Oracle Java AuthTrans functions to execute. See Handle Multiple AuthTrans Functions.

name: EnableWebAgent
description: Specifies whether an agent actively protects resources. See Enable a Web Agent.

name: EncryptAgentName
description: This parameter specifies whether the agent encrypts its name in the URL and whether the credential collector decrypts the name when it receives the URL. See Encrypt the Agent Name.

name: EnforceFQResponseRedirectUrl
description: If set, instructs an agent to use fully qualified domain names (FQDNs) in responses that redirect the user to another URL. See How Response Attributes Work with Web Agents.

name: EnforcePolicies
description: (Domino and IIS 5.x Agents only) Specifies whether an agent actively protects resources.See Starting and Stopping Web Agents.

name: EnforceRealmTimeouts
description: If set, the agent uses WebAgent-OnAuthAccept-Session-Max-Timeout and WebAgent-OnAuthAccept-Session-Idle-Timeout tied to an OnAuthAccept rule to have the realm timeouts change by realm. See How to Enforce Timeouts across Multiple Realms

name: ExpiredCookieURL
description: Specifies a URL to which the agent redirects the user after any session cookie has expired.See Protect Session Cookies from Misuse with Validation Periods and Expired Cookie URLs.

name: ExpireForProxy
description: Prevents a client from caching content (pages and potentially headers or cookies). See Agents and Proxy Servers.

name: FCCCompatMode
description: Allows compatibility with 4.x based Custom Agents used as Credential Collectors (previously named 4xCompatMode). See Use FCCs and NTCs in a Mixed Environment.

name: FCCExt
description: Specifies the default MIME type for the Forms Credential Collector. See Configure MIME Types for Credential Collectors.

name: FCCForceIsProtected
description: Specifies whether the agent makes an additional IsProtected call to the Policy Server to establish a realm context so that the agent can log a user in to access a protected resource. See Force an FCC to Establish Realm Context for Forms Authentication.

name: Fcchtmlencoding
description: Specifies whether the HTML encoding is enabled to prevent Cross-Site Scripting attacks against web agent FCC pages. This parameter does not block any characters. See Prevent Cross-Site Scripting Attacks in Web Agent FCC Pages.

name: ForceCookieDomain
description: If set, forces an agent to append its cookie domain to the host name in a URL request that does not specify a domain or contains only an IP address. This parameter works together with the ForceFQHost parameter for added functionality. See Force the Cookie Domain.

name: ForceFQHost
description: If set, forces an agent to use a fully qualified domain name. See Force the Cookie Domain.

name: ForceGetSessionData
description: (Domino agents only) If set, the agent stores user credentials in the session store to enable user authentication with the web server during single sign-on.

name: ForceIISProxyUser
description: (IIS agents only) If set, the Agent uses an IIS Proxy User. See Use an IIS Proxy User Account.

name: FormCacheTimeout
description: Specifies the number of seconds that an object may reside in the form cache before being considered invalid. See Configure the Form Cache.

name: getcpcookie
description: Controls how requests to cookie provider Agents work with SameSite cookie attribute of Chrome 80. See Configure SiteMinder to Manage the Change in the Default Behavior of Google Chrome 80.

name: GetPortFromHeaders
description: (non-framework Domino agents only) Directs an agent to obtain the port number from the HTTP HOST request header instead of obtaining it from the web server service structures. See Redirect Users to the Correct Port Using the HTTP HOST Request

name: HostConfigFile
description: (Host Configuration File only) Specifies the path to the SMHost.conf file (in an IIS 6.0 or Apache agent). See Parameters Found Only in Local Configuration Files.

name: HTTPHeaderEncodingSpec
description: Affects the encoding of all HTTP header values and all custom HTTP-COOKIE responses. Use this parameter to support the web applications expecting localized text in specific encodings. See Set the HTTP Header Encoding Spec.

name: HTTPServicePrincipal
description: Specifies the web server principal name for Kerberos authentication. See Policy Server Configuration for Kerberos Authentication.

name: HttpsPorts
description: Specifies the ports to treat as SSL even if coming through as HTTP. See Define HTTPS Ports.

name: IdleTimeoutURL
description: Specifies the URL where the agent should redirect the user when the idle time-out for the session occurs. See Redirect a User after a Session Time-out.

name: IgnoreCPForNotprotected
description: If set, prevents the cookie provider from being queried for unprotected resource requests. By default, the agent directs all requests to the cookie provider. See Ignore the Cookie Provider for Unprotected Resources.

name: IgnoreExt
description: Specifies the types of resource requests that the agent passes to the web server without checking access policies. See Reduce Overhead by Ignoring File Extensions of Unprotected Resources.

name: IgnoreHost
description: Specifies the fully qualified domain names of any virtual servers that you want the agent to ignore. Resources on such virtual servers will be automatically authorized. See Specify Virtual Servers to be Ignored by the Web Agent.

name: IgnoreRichStateInURI
description: When protecting WebSphere Portal, set this option to configure the Web Agent to ignore the rich state URL suffix (which begins with “!ut/”) that is generated when friendly URLs are configured. Enable this option to improve Web Agent cache performance.

name: IgnoreQueryData
description: If set, the agent caches the entire URL (including the query strings) and sends the entire URI to the Policy Server for rule processing. See Ignore Query Data.

name: IgnoreUrl
description: Specifies a URI within a URL that is not protected. Users attempting to access the resource associated with the URI will not be challenged. See Allow Unrestricted Access to URIs.

name: IISCacheDisable
description: (IIS Agents only) Specifies whether the IIS web server stores responses containing cookies in an output cache. See Prevent Caching of Server Responses Containing Cookies.

name: IISEnableChildRequest
description: (IIS Agents only) Use only if directed by Broadcom Support to corrects issues with IIS 7 Web Agents.

name: InlineCredentials
description: (IIS Agents only) Specifies how the agent handles user credentials. If set, the agent reads the credentials directly from the HTTP request. By default, the agent redirects to an NTC credential collector. See IIS Web Server Settings.

name: InsecureServer
description: (IIS Agents only) Allows Agents for IIS to enforce native IIS security mechanisms by providing a Windows user security context. See Windows User Security Context.

name: KCCExt
description: Specifies the default MIME type for the Kerberos Credential Collector. See Configure MIME Types for Credential Collectors.

name: LegacyCookieProvider
description: Controls whether an agent redirects a resource request to a traditional agent operating as a cookie provider. See Prevent Non-Get Requests from Being Sent to Cookie Providers in Configure Web Agent Single Sign-On Settings.

name: LegacyEncoding
description: Forces the agent to replace any dollar sign ($) characters in legacy URLs with a hyphen (-). See Accommodate Legacy URL Encoding.

name: LegacyPostPreservationEncoding
description: If set (Yes), the agent encodes any POST preservation data in a way that is compatible with Traditional (Domino and IIS 5.0) agents. See Configure POST Preservation.

name: LegacyStreamingBehavior
description: Specifies how content is transferred to the server during POST requests. See Choose How Content Types are Transferred in POST Requests.

name: LegacyTransferEncoding
description: (Apache agents only) Specifies the type of message encoding that the agent uses. Set this parameter (Yes) to configure the agent to use content encoding to support legacy applications. See Apache Web Server Settings.

name: LegacyVariables
description: If set, the agent uses underscores in HTTP header names. See Enable Legacy Variables for HTTP Headers.

name: LegalHostNameChars
description: Instructs the agent to verify that host names contain only the characters set in this parameter. The agent rejects requests for host names containing any characters that are not specified. See Restrict Host Name Characters.

name: LimitCookieProvider
description: Specifies how the agent acting as a cookie provider handles cookie provider SET requests. See Restrict Cookie Provider Functions.

name: LoadPlugin
description: (WebAgent.conf only) Specifies which plug-ins are loaded for Framework Agents. The plug-ins support different types of Agent functions. See WebAgent.conf file for Framework Agents.

name: LocalConfigfile
description: (WebAgent.conf only) Specifies the location of the LocalConfig.conf file, where most of Agent configuration settings reside. See WebAgent.conf file for Framework Agents.

name: Localization
description: Specifies whether agent internationalization is enabled. Disable to customized FCCs created for use with agent versions prior to 12.51. See FCC Internationalization.

name: LogAppend
description: Adds new log information to the end of an existing log file. By default, the entire log file is rewritten each time logging is invoked. See Set Up and Enable Error Logging.

name: LogFile
description: Turns Error (High Level) Logging on or off. See Set Up and Enable Error Logging.

name: LogFileName
description: Specifies the full path (including the file name) of the log file.See Set Up and Enable Error Logging

name: LogFileName32
description: (IIS only) Specifies the full path (including the file name) of the log file for 32 bit on 64 bit Windows; default is to add _32 before .log. See Set Up and Enable Error Logging.

name: LogFileSize
description: Specifies the size limit of the log file in megabytes. See Set Up and Enable Error Logging.

name: LogFilesToKeep
description: Specifies how many old log files to keep. See Limit the Number of Log Files Saved.

name: LogLocalTime
description: Specifies whether the logs use Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) or local time. See Set Up and Enable Error Logging.

name: LogoffUri
description: Enables the full log-out function by specifying the URI of a custom web page. See How to Configure Full Logoff for Single Sign-On.

name: LowerCaseHTTP
description: (Oracle iPlanet and Domino Agents only) Specifies the case for agent HTTP headers. See Use Lower Case HTTP in Headers.

name: LowerCaseProtocolSpecifier
description: Specifies whether the scheme (protocol) portion of a redirect URL uses only lowercase characters. This configuration parameter accommodates legacy applications that do not conform to RFC 2396. See Specify URL Protocols with Lowercase Characters.

name: MasterCookiePath
description: Specifies the path for the primary-domain session cookies created by the cookie provider. See Specify the Cookie Path for Agent Cookies.

name: MaxResourceCacheSize
description: Specifies the maximum number of entries that Web Agent keeps in its resource cache. An entry contains the Policy Server response and any additional attributes. See Set the Maximum Resource Cache Size.

name: MaxSessionCacheSize
description: Specifies the maximum number of users the Agent maintains in its session cache. See Set the Maximum User Session Cache Size.

name: MaxTimeoutURL
description: Specifies the URL where the agent redirects the user when the maximum time-out for the session occurs. See Redirect a User after a Session Time-out.

name: MaxUrlSize
description: Specifies the maximum size (in bytes) of a URL that an agent can handle. See URL Settings.

name: MaxAuthorizationCacheSize
description: Controls the authorization of Agent cache independently. The values defined in this ACO property are used to initialize the cache. See Web Agent Caches.

name: NTCExt
description: Specifies the default extension for the NT Credential Collector. See Specify an NTLM Credential Collector.

name: OverlookSessionAsPattern
description: If enabled, the agent does not create cookies for any of the URLs under the directory that is specified in OverlookSessionForUrls. See Prevent Session Cookie Creation or Updates.

name: OverlookSessionForMethods
description: Specifies a list of methods against which the agent compares the request method of all HTTP requests. If a match occurs, the agent does not create or update an SMSESSION cookie. See Prevent Session Cookie Creation or Updates.

name: OverlookSessionForMethodUri
description: Specifies whether the agent compares the method and the URI from all HTTP requests against the method and URI listed in this parameter. If a match occurs, the gent does not create or update an SMSESSION cookie. See Prevent Session Cookie Creation or Updates Based on Method and URI.

name: OverlookSessionForUrls
description: Specifies a list of URLs against which the agent compares the URLs from all HTTP requests. If a match occurs, the agent does not create or update an SMSESSION cookie. See Prevent Session Cookie Creation or Updates.

name: OverrideIgnoreExtFilter
description: Specifies a list of strings you want the agent to match against all URIs. This helps you protect resources whose extensions are normally ignored by the agent. See Protect Resources Without Extensions.

name: OverlookSessionForQuery
description: Defines strings which can appear as substrings of the URL’s query. This parameter ignores the Ajax call based on the attributes in the query string. See Session Cookie Management.

name: P3PCompactPolicy
description: Determines whether custom responses comply with the Platform for Privacy Preferences Project (P3P) response headers. See Configure your Web Agent to Accommodate P3P Compact Policies.

name: PersistentCookies
description: Specifies whether the agent sets persistent cookies. See Set Persistent Cookies.

name: PersistentIPCheck
description: Enables IP checking for persistent cookies. See Compare IP Addresses to Prevent Security Breaches

name: PostPreservationFile
description: Enables the transfer of POST preservation data between Traditional and Framework Agents. See Enable Post Preservation between Framework and Traditional Agents.

name: PreserveHeaders
description: Specifies whether the agent preserves headers. See Preserve HTTP Headers.

name: PreservePostData
description: Specifies whether the agent preserves POST data when redirecting requests. See Enable or Disable POST Preservation.

name: ProxyAgent
description: Specifies whether an agent acts as a reverse proxy agent. See SiteMinder Reverse Proxy Deployment Considerations.

name: ProxyDefinition
description: Specifies the IP address of a proxy (such as a cache device) that requires the use of a custom HTTP header. See Default HTTP Headers Used by the Product.

name: ProxyHeadersAutoAuth
description: Specifies the value of an HTTP 1.1 header that the agent inserts into an HTTP response to a client. See Customize the Cache-Control and ExpireForProxy Header Settings.

name: ProxyHeadersAutoAuth10
description: Specifies the value of an HTTP 1.0 header that the agent inserts into an HTTP response to a client. See Customize the Cache-Control and ExpireForProxy Header Settings.

name: ProxyHeadersDefaultTime
description: Sets the max-age cache header parameter when a value hasn't been provided via the other cache header parameters, such as ProxyHeadersAutoAuth. See Customize the Cache-Control and ExpireForProxy Header Settings.

name: ProxyHeadersTimeoutPercentage
description: Percent of session timeout value to be used as cache header timeout (max-age). See Customize the Cache-Control and ExpireForProxy Header Settings

name: ProxyHeadersProtected
description: Specifies the value of an HTTP 1.1 header that the agent inserts into an HTTP response to a client. See Customize the Cache-Control and ExpireForProxy Header Settings.

name: ProxyHeadersProtected10
description: Specifies the value of an HTTP 1.0 header that the agent inserts into an HTTP response to a client. See Customize the Cache-Control and ExpireForProxy Header Settings.

name: ProxyHeadersUnprotected
description: Specifies the value of an HTTP 1.1 header that the agent inserts into an HTTP response to a client. See Customize the Cache-Control and ExpireForProxy Header Settings.

name: ProxyHeadersUnprotected10
description: Specifies the value of an HTTP 1.0 header that the agent inserts into an HTTP response to a client. See Customize the Cache-Control and ExpireForProxy Header Settings.

name: ProxyTimeout
description: Specifies the number of seconds the reverse proxy server waits for the agent that is deployed behind it to respond to a request. See How to Configure an Apache Reverse Proxy Server.

name: ProxyTrust
description: Instructs the agent on a destination server to trust authorizations received from an agent on a proxy server. See Agents and Proxy Servers.

name: PSPollInterval
description: Specifies how often (in seconds) the agent contacts the Policy Server to retrieve information about policy changes or dynamically updated keys. See Change How Often an Agent Checks for Policy or Key Updates.

name: preserveuniversalID
description: Specifies the Universal ID set to non-protected resources when a valid SMSESSION cookie is available.

name: RemoteUserVar
description: Instructs the agent to use a non-default (SMUSER or SM_USER) variable for REMOTE_USER. See Configure the Web Agent to set the REMOTE_USER Variable.

name: ReqCookieErrorFile
description: Specifies the file to use when cookies are not enabled and required. See Set Up Error Handling.

name: RequireClientIP
description: If set, forces the client to have an IP Address that is found for IP Checking. See Configure IP Address Validation.

name: RequireCookies
description: Specifies whether SiteMinder requires cookies. See Require Cookies for Basic Authentication.

name: ResourceCacheTimeout
description: Specifies the number of seconds that resource entries remain in the cache. See Control How Long Resource Entries Remain Cached.

name: SaveCredsTimeout
description: Specifies the number of hours that a persistent cookie containing the user credentials will be saved. See Set a Timeout for Saved Credentials.

name: SACExt
description: Specifies the default extension that is used in the session assurance flow. See Configure Enhanced Session Assurance with DeviceDNA™.

name: Samesite
description: Controls the behavior of SameSite cookie attribute. See Configure SiteMinder to Manage the Change in the Default Behavior of Google Chrome 80.

name: samesiteincompatibleuseragents
description: Determines which web browser and its versions must not contain the SameSite cookie attribute. See Configure SiteMinder to Manage the Change in the Default Behavior of Google Chrome 80.

name: SCCExt
description: Specifies the extension of the Secure Credential Collector (SCC). See Configure MIME Types for Credential Collectors.

name: SecureApps
description: If set, prevents the agent from authorizing URLs from an unauthorized user via fake URLs. See Secure Applications.

name: SecureURLs
description: If set, secures URLs by encrypting the query string. See Configure SecureUrls with Single Sign-on.

name: ServerErrorFile
description: Specifies the file to use if there is a server error. See Set Up Error Handling.

name: SessionGracePeriod
description: Specifies the number of seconds before the agent regenerates a session cookie. See Modify the Session Grace Period.

name: SessionUpdatePeriod
description: Specifies how often (in seconds) an agent redirects a request to the Cookie Provider to set a new cookie. See Modify the Session Update Period.

name: SetRemoteUser
description: If set, Instructs the agent to set the REMOTE_USER variable. See Configure the Web Agent to set the REMOTE_USER Variable.

name: SFCCExt
description: Specifies the extension of the Secure Forms Credential Collector (SFCC). See Configure MIME Types for Credential Collectors.

name: SharedSecret
description: (SmHost.conf) Maintained for backwards compatibility only.

name: SkipDominoAuth
description: (Domino and IIS 5.x Agents only) If set, configures SiteMinder (and not Domino) to authenticate users. See Authenticate Users with the Domino Server.

name: SmpsServicePrincipal
description: Specifies the Policy Server principal name for Kerberos authentication. See Policy Server Configuration for Kerberos Authentication.

name: SSOTrustedZone
description: Specifies an ordered list of all trusted SSO zones by name. Enter the zone names in the order that the agent must search for session cookies. See The Order of Trust and Failover..

name: SSOZoneName
description: Specifies the name of an SSO zone. See Security Zones for Single Sign-On.

name: StoreSessioninServer
description: If set, configures agents and cookie providers to store the session temporarily and pass a GUID that identifies the stored session instead of the session cookie in the redirect URL. See Enable Single Use Session Cookies.

name: SuppressServerHeader
description: (IIS Agent only) Prevents an agent from returning the Server HTTP Header in its responses. See Remove the Server HTTP Header if Using the URLScan Utility.

name: TargetAsRelativeURI
description: If set, the target parameter that is appended to the credential collector URL is a relative target. In turn, when the credential collector redirects back to the Web Agent protecting the target resource, it is a relative redirect. See Use a Relative Target for Credential Collector Redirects.

name: TraceAppend
description: If set, the agent adds new logging information to the end of an existing log file instead of rewriting the entire file each time logging is invoked. See Configure Trace Logging.

name: TraceConfigFile
description: Specifies the location of the WebAgentTrace.conf configuration file that determines which components and events to monitor. See Configure Trace Logging.

name: TraceConfigFile32
description: (IIS Agent only) Specifies the location of the WebAgentTrace.conf configuration file that determines which components and events to monitor. See Configure Trace Logging

name: TraceDelimiter
description: Specifies a custom character that separates the fields in the trace file. See Configure Trace Logging.

name: TraceFile
description: Specifies whether the agent writes a trace (Low Level) file. See Configure Trace Logging.

name: TraceFileName
description: Specifies the full path to the trace log file. See Configure Trace Logging.

name: TraceFileName32
description: (IIS Agent Only) Specifies the full path to the trace file for an Agent for IIS that is running on a 64-bit Windows operating environment and protecting 32-bit applications. See Configure Trace Logging.

name: TraceFileSize
description: Specifies (in megabytes) the maximum size of a trace file. See Configure Trace Logging.

name: TraceFilesToKeep
description: Specifies the number of old trace files to keep. See Limit the Number of Trace Log Files Saved.

name: TraceFormat
description: Specifies how the trace file displays the messages. See Configure Trace Logging.

name: TrackCPSessionDomain
description: If set, the agent validates that the cookie domain of the session cookie matches the cookie domain of the cookie provider. See Prevent Cookie Provider Replay Attacks.

name: TrackSessionDomain
description: If set, the agent encrypts and stores the intended domain of a session cookie within the session cookie itself. See Validate a Session Cookie Domain.

name: TransientIDCookies
description: If set, the agent identity cookie (SMIDENTITY) is transient. See Control Identity Cookies.

name: TransientIPCheck
description: If set, compare the IP address stored in a transient cookie from the last request against the IP address contained in the current request. See Compare IP Addresses to Prevent Security Breaches.

name: trustediplistfornegotiateauthentication
description: If set, collects the IP address range of a client. If the IP address of a client is within the range, the client is authenticated using the Windows authentication scheme. If the IP address of a client is outside the range, the client is authenticated using the Forms authentication scheme. See Configure IWA Fallback to Forms Using Authentication Chain.

name: UseAnonAccess
description: (IIS Agent only) If set, the agent executes the web application as an anonymous user, instead of using credentials of the proxy user. See Enable Anonymous User Access.

name: UseDominoUserForUnprotected
description: (Domino Agents only) If set, the Domino server authenticates requests with a Domino user for resources that only the Domino server (not SiteMinder) protects. See Force the Domino server to authenticate unprotected resources.

name: UseHttpOnlyCookies
description: If set, the agent sets the HTTP-only attribute on the cookies it creates. See Safeguard Information in Cookies with HTTP-Only Attribute.

name: UseNetBIOSforIISAuth
description: (IIS 6.0 Agent only) Specifies whether the agent sends the user principal name (UPN) or the NetBIOS name to the IIS 6.0 web server for IIS user authentication. SeeUse the NetBIOS Name or UPN for IIS Authentication.

name: UseNtlmMapForNtlmAuth
description: If set to Yes, this parameter prevents the Access Gateway server from crashing when the load balancer does not honor the sticky-bit configuration. See Access Gateway Troubleshooting for more information.

name: UseSecureCookies
description: If set, the agent sets the HTTPS-only attribute on the cookies it creates. See Set Secure Cookies.

name: UseSecureCPCookies
description: If set, the cookie provider will only send a cookie to an agent in another cookie domain that is also configured to use secure cookies (that is, for which UseSecureCookies is enabled). See Set Secure Cookies Across Multiple Domains.

name: UseServerRequestIp
description: If set, the agent resolves the AgentName according to the physical IP address of a virtual web server. See .

name: UseSessionForAnonymous
description: Specifies whether an agent uses the session from an available SMSESSION cookie when accessing a resource protected by anonymous authentication. See User Identity and Activity Tracking and URL Monitoring.

name: UserLookupFromDN
description: When certificate or token based authentication schemes are used, this parameter defines the username value. If this parameter is set to cn or uid, the user name is extracted from the userDN and this value is set as SM_USER value. See UserLookupFromDN Examples.

name: URITransform
description: Defines the expression(s) for performing the transformation of the URI into a suitable form based on expression. The transformed URI is treated as a resource in the IsProtected call which triggers the realm access policy at Policy Server. See Resource Transformation.

name: ValidFedTargetDomain
description: Defines valid domains for your federated environment See Agent Setting for Federation Domains.

name: ValidTargetDomain
description: Defines the domains to which a credential collector is allowed to redirect users. If the domain in the URL does not match the domains set in this parameter, the redirect is denied. See Define Valid Target Domains

name: WebAppClientResponse
description: Configures responses to web service clients. See Apply SiteMinder Behavior to a Web Application Client.

name: XFrameOptions
description: Instructs the agent to preserve the X-Frame-Options header when it matches. See Ensure Custom Responses Comply with X-Frame Options.

name: xforwardedproto
description: (For Web Agent 12.8) Supports the X-Forwarded-Proto request header that identifies the protocol that a client uses to connect to a load balancer. Web Agent uses the same protocol that is included in this request header in its response to the client. See X-Forwarded-Proto Header Support.
